summary: Help‑Slip‑Franklin magic, seamless second set, rare Attics encore
blurb: The show opens with a goose‑bumps “Help On The Way” that launches an unforgettable Help>Slip>Franklin sequence. A fluid second set weaves Playin’ In The Band into China Doll → Uncle John's Band → Terrapin Station, and the night caps with the rare Attics Of My Life encore. Jerry’s tone shines, Phil drives the groove, and Brent delivers a scorching organ solo on Little Red Rooster.
review: March 30, 1990 at Nassau Coliseum delivered one of those spring‑time Dead shows that still feels fresh. From the first note of “Help On The Way,” Jerry Garcia’s guitar sparked goose‑bumps, setting up a flawless Help>Slip>Franklin chain that many fans still cite as a high point of the era. The second set was a masterclass in flow – Playin’ In The Band segued into China Doll, then Uncle John's Band and rolled straight into an expansive Terrapin Station, all underpinned by Phil’s inspired bass lines and Mickey & Billy’s tight, pre‑drums magic. Brent Mydland’s organ roared on Little Red Rooster and he kept the harmonic landscape lush throughout. The night closed with the seldom‑played “Attics Of My Life,” giving collectors a rare encore treat. Across five available sources, the FM broadcast (gd1990-03-30.fm.greider-nawrocki.3390.sbefail.shnf) stands out for its crystal‑clear mix and balanced soundstage, making it the go‑to version for anyone wanting to hear this show at its best.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are overwhelmingly positive, praising the Help>Slip>Franklin sequence, seamless second‑set segues, and rare Attics encore; only one source is mixed, no negative consensus appears.

best_recording.reason: FM broadcast delivers an excellent, crisp mix that captures the goose‑bumps opener and rare Attics encore with pristine clarity.

key_highlights: Help On The Way > Slipknot > Franklin's Tower sequence ignites the night
Playin' In The Band → China Doll → Uncle John's Band seamless segue
Terrapin Station extended jam backed by tight pre‑drums magic
Rare Attics Of My Life encore adds historic value

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is on fire from the opening Help, delivering soaring leads and a sweet guitar voice throughout; a few mixes bury his vocals but his playing remains the show’s emotional core.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides an inspired, solid bass foundation that drives the band’s energy, especially during the high‑octane second set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir offers steady rhythm work and dependable vocals, with his All Over Now solo noted as cut short on one source but otherwise contributing to the groove.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines with an intense B‑3 organ solo on Little Red Rooster and a rich keyboard presence that underpins every transition.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming blends seamlessly into the pre‑drums segment, adding texture and momentum to the extended jams.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in with Hart, delivering solid, inspiring rhythms that keep the second set flowing.
